---
title: "summarize_forecast"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


### summarize_forecast()

**Purpose:**  
Creates a short summary of a forecast result.

**What it does:**  
This function pulls out the starting value, ending value, total contributions, and gain from a forecast data frame. It is useful for quick reporting.

**Use case:**  
Use this to summarize investment, savings, or loan results in one clean table.

**Inputs:**  
- `forecast`: A data frame returned by a forecasting function.
- `initial`: The starting amount.

**Output:**  
A one-row data frame with summary values.

**Example:**  
`summary_forecast(grow_investment(1000, 0.07, 10, 500), 1000)`
